name: Project SOP
description: The Project SOP creates curves on surface (also known as trim or profile curves) by projecting a 3D face onto a spline surface, much like a light casts a 2D shadow onto a 3D surface. There are two projection methods: along a vector, or by mapping the face directly onto the parametric space of the surface.
summary: The Project SOP creates curves on surface (also known as trim or profile curves) by projecting a 3D face onto a spline surface, much like a light casts a 2D shadow onto a 3D surface. There are two pro

parameters.name: Face Group
parameters.label: Face Group
parameters.description: The group of faces to be projected onto the spline surfaces.

parameters.name: Surface Group
parameters.label: Surface Group
parameters.description: surfgroup - The group of surfaces to project faces onto. Accepts patterns, as described in Pattern Matching.

parameters.name: Cycle Type
parameters.label: Cycle Type
parameters.description:
cycle -  - Select the method use to project the 3D faces onto the 3D spline surfaces.

 allseq - If projected parametrically, the spatial relationship between the faces will be preserved. For example, if the text "hello" is projected parametrically and in sequence, the letters will appear on the surface naturally, side by side. Sequence is not applicable if projecting along a vector. allover - If projected parametrically, the spatial relationship between the faces is not preserved, so they will appear overlapped in the area of the domain chosen for projection. Overlapping is not applicable if projecting along a vector. oneeach - Each face is projected onto a surface in the order in which the face and the surface appear in the input or in their respective groups. cycled - This is the same as One Per Surface, above, but starts the faces again if there are surfaces left.

parameters.name: All on Each Surface in Sequence
parameters.label: All on Each Surface in Sequence
parameters.description: allseq - If projected parametrically, the spatial relationship between the faces will be preserved. For example, if the text "hello" is projected parametrically and in sequence, the letters will appear on the surface naturally, side by side. Sequence is not applicable if projecting along a vector. allover - If projected parametrically, the spatial relationship between the faces is not preserved, so they will appear overlapped in the area of the domain chosen for projection. Overlapping is not applicable if projecting along a vector. oneeach - Each face is projected onto a surface in the order in which the face and the surface appear in the input or in their respective groups. cycled - This is the same as One Per Surface, above, but starts the faces again if there are surfaces left.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description:
method -  - Select between projecting along a vector or parametrically using the parameters below.

 vector - The face is projected along a 3D vector and its image on the surface is converted into a curve on the surface. One spatial curve may generate several profiles depending on its position relative to the surface, the shape of the surface, and the chosen projection side. If the projected face does not intersect the surface at all, no profile curve will be generated. parametric - The spatial size of the faces is mapped directly onto the domain of the spline surface. The resulting projection is sensitive to the parameterization of the surface and is likely to appear distorted. It is, however, the fastest of the two projection types and tends to behave well on surfaces with regular shapes and chord-length parameterizations.

parameters.name: Along Vector
parameters.label: Along Vector
parameters.description: vector - The face is projected along a 3D vector and its image on the surface is converted into a curve on the surface. One spatial curve may generate several profiles depending on its position relative to the surface, the shape of the surface, and the chosen projection side. If the projected face does not intersect the surface at all, no profile curve will be generated. parametric - The spatial size of the faces is mapped directly onto the domain of the spline surface. The resulting projection is sensitive to the parameterization of the surface and is likely to appear distorted. It is, however, the fastest of the two projection types and tends to behave well on surfaces with regular shapes and chord-length parameterizations.

parameters.name: Axis
parameters.label: Axis
parameters.description:
axis -  - The axis along which the four corners of the feature are projected onto the base. When adding the feature from the outside as a Vector paste.

 xaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. yaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. zaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. fnorm - Projection axis occurs along each face's normal. Thus, each face could potentially have a different projection direction than the others. The option also works well for non-planar faces. mindist - Projects the entire 3D face along the vectors of minimum distance to the surface. The projection resolution is given by the  value. other - Enables the Vector fields immediately below to specify the X, Y, and Z components of the vector.Note: When the Minimum Distance option is selected, the Side button becomes disabled because it's irrelevant for this projection type.

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: xaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. yaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. zaxis - Cartesian axis X, Y, or Z. fnorm - Projection axis occurs along each face's normal. Thus, each face could potentially have a different projection direction than the others. The option also works well for non-planar faces. mindist - Projects the entire 3D face along the vectors of minimum distance to the surface. The projection resolution is given by the  value. other - Enables the Vector fields immediately below to specify the X, Y, and Z components of the vector.Note: When the Minimum Distance option is selected, the Side button becomes disabled because it's irrelevant for this projection type.

parameters.name: Vector
parameters.label: Vector
parameters.description:
vector -  - The X, Y, and Z components of the projection vector if none of the main axes is chosen in the Axis parameter above.

vector1 -vector2 -vector3 -

parameters.name: Side
parameters.label: Side
parameters.description:
projside -  - Select which sides of the geometry to project on, closest or farthest.

 closest - Will project the face onto the closest part of the surface in either direction of projection vector. For example, if a curve being projected onto a tube is inside the tube, it will yield two profiles, one on each side of the tube; if the curve is outside the tube, it will project onto the side closest to it. farthest - The reverse of Closest. In the example above, Farthest would also yield two images if the curve is inside the tube, but it will choose the farthest side if the curve is outside the tube.

parameters.name: Closest
parameters.label: Closest
parameters.description: closest - Will project the face onto the closest part of the surface in either direction of projection vector. For example, if a curve being projected onto a tube is inside the tube, it will yield two profiles, one on each side of the tube; if the curve is outside the tube, it will project onto the side closest to it. farthest - The reverse of Closest. In the example above, Farthest would also yield two images if the curve is inside the tube, but it will choose the farthest side if the curve is outside the tube.

parameters.name: Divisions per Span
parameters.label: Divisions per Span
parameters.description: sdivs - The number of points to be computed on the spatial face between successive spans. A span is the line connecting two consecutive CVs on a polygon, or the arc between two breakpoints on a spline curve. The projection tends to become more accurate as the number of divisions increases.

parameters.name: Ray Tolerance
parameters.label: Ray Tolerance
parameters.description: rtolerance - Controls the precision of the ray intersection with the surface. The ray is cast along the projection vector from every point of the 3-D curve.

parameters.name: Fit Tolerance
parameters.label: Fit Tolerance
parameters.description: ftolerance - Controls the 2-D fitting precision and is typically less than 0.01.

parameters.name: Max UV Gap (%)
parameters.label: Max UV Gap (%)
parameters.description: uvgap - This specifies what percentage of the size of the surface domain is acceptable for two separate profiles to be joined into a single curve.

parameters.name: Order
parameters.label: Order
parameters.description: order - The spline order of the resulting profile curve. The type of curve (Bzier or NURBS) is inherited from the spatial curve. The order, however, is not inherited because the spline order provides useful control over the quality of the fit. If the spatial face is a polygon, the profile will be a NURBS curve.

parameters.name: Preserve Sharp Corners
parameters.label: Preserve Sharp Corners
parameters.description: csharp - Controls the precision with which sharp corners in the projection curve are interpolated. It should be on when the projection has areas of high changes in curvature.

parameters.name: Super Accurate Projection
parameters.label: Super Accurate Projection
parameters.description: accurate - Use a very accurate yet expensive algebraic pruning algorithm to determine the intersection of the vector with the surface.

parameters.name: U from
parameters.label: U from
parameters.description:
ufrom -  - Specifies which of the spatial coordinates - X, Y, or Z - must be mapped to the U parametric direction of the surface.

 uvx - uvy - uvz -

parameters.name: X Coordinate
parameters.label: X Coordinate
parameters.description: uvx - uvy - uvz -

parameters.name: V from
parameters.label: V from
parameters.description:
vfrom -  - Specifies which of the spatial coordinates - X, Y, or Z - must be mapped to the V parametric direction of the surface.

 uvx - uvy - uvz -

parameters.name: Map Profile to Range:
parameters.label: Map Profile to Range:
parameters.description: Map Profile to Range: parameter.

parameters.name: U Range
parameters.label: U Range
parameters.description:
urange -  - Indicates in percentages what part of the U surface domain is the mapping area. A full range of 0-1 will cause the profiles to be mapped to the entire domain in the U parametric direction. The range is not restricted to the 0-1 interval.

urange1 -urange2 -

parameters.name: V Range
parameters.label: V Range
parameters.description:
vrange -  - Indicates in percentages what part of the V surface domain is the mapping area. A full range of 0-1 will cause the profiles to be mapped to the entire domain in the V parametric direction. The range is not restricted to the 0-1 interval.

vrange1 -vrange2 -

parameters.name: Mapping Type
parameters.label: Mapping Type
parameters.description:
maptype -  - Select how to reposition and scale an existing profile to fit within the specified domain range.

 unif - Mapping converts the spatial coordinates of the projection faces to (U,V) points in the domain of the surface without taking into account the parameterization of the surface. chordlen - Mapping takes into account the surface parameterization and attempts to compute a projection best suited to the spatial and parametric determinants of the model.If the face is not mapped to range, it's vertices will be transferred to the profile without any transformation. Use this technique together with the parametric extraction in the Profile  to edit the points of a profile generated by a  other than Project: extract, edit the points, then project again.

parameters.name: Uniform
parameters.label: Uniform
parameters.description: unif - Mapping converts the spatial coordinates of the projection faces to (U,V) points in the domain of the surface without taking into account the parameterization of the surface. chordlen - Mapping takes into account the surface parameterization and attempts to compute a projection best suited to the spatial and parametric determinants of the model.If the face is not mapped to range, it's vertices will be transferred to the profile without any transformation. Use this technique together with the parametric extraction in the Profile  to edit the points of a profile generated by a  other than Project: extract, edit the points, then project again.
